task: Add the next Pro Rust product-core slice: a keyless execution readiness gate that composes the existing workspace access gate with provider-adapter gate checks and worker/result-commit blockers before any live provider, credential, quota, worker, or result-write execution can be enabled. Expose one local API endpoint, render one graph-first web panel, and synchronize Pro docs/evidence. This remains preview-only and must not add provider calls, credentials, auth enforcement, quota mutation, billing, worker execution, package publishing, npm dependencies, or OSS runtime changes.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
Do not accept or store passwords, tokens, provider secrets, or API keys.
Execution readiness must deny live/provider/worker/result-write paths until hosted gates exist.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain why this gate composes existing boundary vocabulary instead of enabling execution.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing workspace access-gate, provider-adapter gate vocabulary, Axum endpoint style, and compact web panel patterns.
patternSummary: Reuse the existing Pro domain gate payloads, API route handlers, web fetchJson helpers, and preview-only boundary panels.
smallestViableChange: Add a deterministic execution readiness payload that reports live execution denied until workspace, provider, vault, quota, worker, and result-commit gates are satisfied.
assumptions: The Pro runtime remains local preview-only.
Real provider/search execution is still intentionally blocked.
acceptanceCriteria: Domain tests prove preview execution readiness denies live provider calls while showing satisfied preview observations and remaining hosted blockers.
API tests prove the endpoint returns a server-computed readiness payload and no secret-shaped fields.
Web tests prove the graph-first shell contains the readiness panel, endpoint path, and renderer.
Docs and evidence explain that this is not real provider execution or auth enforcement.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless execution readiness endpoint.
Pro web shell renders whether live execution is still denied and which gates block it.
riskJustification: This is a keyless local readiness evaluator that composes existing denied gates. It prepares an explicit execution boundary without enabling provider calls, credential reads, quota reservations, worker execution, or result writes.
